verdict_reasoning: SRC is a canonical cytoplasmic tyrosine kinase with myristoylation at its N-terminus, conferring inner-leaflet PM anchoring under baseline conditions. However, SRC is documented to translocate to the outer leaflet and/or be displayed on the cell surface in multiple non-baseline contexts: (1) cancer cells show SRC ecto-kinase activity and outer-leaflet presentation, particularly in solid tumors and metastatic disease; (2) apoptotic and necroptotic cells externalize SRC during programmed cell death; (3) activated immune cells (T cells, NK cells) show SRC surface display during activation-induced degranulation and regulated exocytosis. These cell-state-induced and activation-linked surface presentations qualify as contextual surface accessibility, distinct from the baseline cytoplasmic-inner-leaflet anchoring.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent of cancer-cell ecto-SRC display and activation-induced immune-cell surface SRC are well-documented in specialized contexts but baseline SRC is canonically inner-leaflet-anchored cytoplasmic kinase; verdict hinges on whether non-baseline ecto-presentation in transformed and activated cells is sufficient to rate as contextual surface accessibility.
